reset_note: FRESH LAUNCH from scratch under the 2026-07-06 rules (whole-universe forced-seller sourcing + primary-source verification gate). Operator directive: forget all prior dossier choices and start clean. This is NOT a buy-launch — it tees up VERIFIED choices for when funding lands. The prior pool (9 dossiers incl. the 4 survivors + 4 launch-gate kills) is archived in oracle_convex_dossiers_archive_pre_reset_2026_07_06.json for the audit trail; the learning survives in the verification gate's four traps + RESEARCH_LEDGER. Legacy cohort (CXT/HDSN/J/PSN/VITL) untouched — operator-held, not the engine's.
source: fresh launch: first verified name — sourced by forced_seller_sourcing (form-enum), cleared by verify_dossier | +INVE (neglect leg, 2026-07-06): 2nd verified name, post-divestiture cash box ~29% below net cash, primary-source 10-Q verified. | +XBIT/SEER/LAB (neglect leg 27-name verification pass 2026-07-06): 3 more primary-source-verified sub-net-cash floors. | +FPH (BLIND fresh-run find 2026-07-06): the cold re-run's own FUND — a CA land bank at ~0.45x an understated book, floor+activist catalyst.

JOF.author: oracle
JOF.business: Japan Smaller Capitalization Fund, Inc. — a US-listed (NYSE) closed-end fund (Nomura-advised) holding a diversified portfolio of Japanese small-cap equities; audited daily NAV.
JOF.thesis: JOF trades at $11.68 against a published, audited daily NAV of $13.04 — a live −10.4% discount, not an asserted one. A US-listed vehicle for Japanese small-caps is structurally orphaned (no natural US buyer base for foreign small-caps), so the discount is persistent — but it is GOVERNED: a standing board Conditional Tender Offer auto-tenders up to 10% of shares at 98% of NAV whenever the average discount exceeds 9% over the annual measurement window, which caps how wide the discount can run and periodically compresses it toward NAV.
JOF.why_mispriced: A US-listed Japan-small-cap CEF is a structurally orphaned vehicle — no natural US holder base — so it sits at a persistent ~10% discount to a real, daily-published NAV, with a board discount-control tender that forces periodic compression a passive holder pool won't.
JOF.catalyst: Recurring Conditional Tender Offer: the >9%-average-discount trigger fired this year at 10.5% and, with the discount still −10.4%, is likely to trigger again in the next measurement window; plus a re-leveled ~10% managed distribution ($0.0956/mo) that itself pressures the discount narrower.
JOF.falsifiable_prediction: The discount to NAV narrows from −10.4% toward ≤3% (via the next CTO trigger and/or the managed distribution) within ~15 months, while NAV is marked daily above the price.
JOF.kill_condition: Exit on a 15% drawdown from entry (the floor — a NAV/yen risk-off broke the thesis), or if the board abandons the Conditional Tender Offer discount-control policy.
JOF.adversarial: Honest bear case: the IMMEDIATE tender window closed 7/1/26, so the near-term forcing event is spent and the next is ~a year out and conditional; the floor is Japan-equity + yen BETA, not contractual — NAV itself can fall 15-20% in a risk-off; post-tender proration returns unsold shares as mild overhang; and the CTO CAPS the discount (~9-10%) rather than erasing it, so the discount can linger between tenders. Convexity is real but MODEST — a floor-plus-small-upside value play, not a multibagger.
citations: JOF SC TO-I (tender offer), filed 2026-06-01, accession 0001140361-26-023437
JOF N-CSR (FY ended 2026-02-28; CTO policy + 9% trigger), filed 2026-05-05, accession 0001133228-26-007999
JOF tender expiration/preliminary results PR, GlobeNewswire 2026-07-02 (22.0M shares tendered, prorated, 98% of NAV)
NAV/discount: CEFConnect JOF — NAV $13.04, price $11.68, discount −10.43% as of 2026-07-02

verification.notes: Primary-source verified: SC TO-I + N-CSR confirm the recurring 9%-trigger CTO at 98% NAV; NAV $13.04 vs $11.68 = live −10.4% discount (CEFConnect, N-CSR framework). Only real archetype of 7 sourced CEF/BDC leads; 6 were preferred-leverage tenders, non-traded at-NAV redemptions, or illiquid marked-down BDCs.

INVE.author: oracle
INVE.business: Identiv, Inc. — after divesting its Physical Access and Credentials/identity businesses for cash, a small IoT/RFID inlay & specialty-tag maker sitting on a large cash pile (revenue ~$7.4M/quarter).
INVE.thesis: After selling its physical-access and identity businesses for cash, Identiv is a ~$63M-cap company holding $124.5M cash and ZERO debt, trading ~29% below net cash on a fully-diluted, net-of-all-liabilities basis. The market misprices it because it is small and sell-side-uncovered and its remaining money-losing IoT/RFID stub makes quant and index screens read the P&L losses rather than the debt-free balance sheet — nobody is forced to close the cash gap.
INVE.why_mispriced: Post-divestiture cash box: a small, uncovered stock whose money-losing operating stub makes screens read the losses not the debt-free $124.5M balance sheet; no analyst, no index, no forced buyer to close the gap.
INVE.catalyst: Capital return (existing $10M repurchase authorization; potential larger return), an accretive redeployment of the cash the market credits, or an activist forcing realization of the sub-net-cash discount.
INVE.falsifiable_prediction: Within ~18 months INVE re-rates toward net cash (>= $3.30/sh) OR the net-cash-per-FD-share floor holds >= $3.30 while price lags.
INVE.kill_condition: Net cash per fully-diluted share falls below $3.00 (accelerated burn or a value-destroying acquisition), or a definitive agreement to spend the cash on an unrelated >$40M acquisition at a premium to book.
INVE.kill_condition_value: 10-Q shows net cash per FD share < $3.00, or a >$40M unrelated acquisition
INVE.adversarial: The house case against: a value trap — a sub-$5 microcap cash box can sit ~30% below net cash for years (dead money); the operating stub bleeds ~$14M/yr, slowly melting the floor; the 7.13M as-converted Series B dilutes the common claim ~23% (the headline 49%-below-net-cash screen is really ~29% fully diluted); management controls the cash and could torch it on a bad deal; and there is NO dated catalyst forcing realization.
citations: SEC 10-Q Identiv Inc, filed 2026-05-14, accession 0001193125-26-223705 (inve-20260331.htm): cash & equivalents $124.525M, restricted cash $0.3M, total liabilities $10.482M, ZERO debt/credit facility; Series B preferred as-converted 7,126,723 sh; common outstanding 24,006,212 sh (as of 2026-05-04); no goodwill (intangibles $0); no noncontrolling interests (wholly-owned subs).

verification.notes: Primary-source verified against the 2026-03-31 10-Q. Floor is HARD: $124.5M cash, zero debt, $10.5M all-operating liabilities, no goodwill, no minority interests. Net-of-everything cash = $114.0M; fully diluted 31.13M sh (24.0M common + 7.13M as-converted Series B) => $3.66/sh vs $2.61 price = ~29% below net cash. The 4 traps pass on FLOOR integrity; the CATALYST is the weak leg (undated neglect) — size as a bounded-floor position, not a top-conviction catalyst bet.

XBIT.author: oracle
XBIT.business: XBiotech Inc. — True Human antibody developer; sold bermekimab to Janssen (2019) for a large cash sum, owns its Austin manufacturing facility; small clinical pipeline.
XBIT.thesis: XBiotech holds $115.5M cash and ZERO debt against a ~$70M market cap — trading ~37% below net cash (25% fully diluted, all options out-of-the-money). The market ignores the debt-free balance sheet because it is a small, uncovered clinical-stage biotech with no product revenue; yet management has a documented, repeated history of returning the Janssen windfall to shareholders.
XBIT.why_mispriced: Post-Janssen-windfall cash box: a small, sell-side-uncovered biotech whose clinical losses make screens read the P&L not the $115.5M debt-free cash; no index, no forced buyer.
XBIT.catalyst: Recurring capital return — management ran self-tenders (Feb-2020, May-2023) and a special dividend (Jul-2021) with the Janssen cash; another tender/dividend would close the discount. Currently dormant.
XBIT.falsifiable_prediction: Within ~18 months XBIT re-rates toward net cash (>= $3.10/sh) OR a new tender/dividend returns cash.
XBIT.kill_condition: Net cash per share falls below $2.50 (burn accelerates with no capital return), or cash is committed to a dilutive acquisition.
XBIT.kill_condition_value: 10-Q net cash/sh < $2.50 or a dilutive M&A use of cash
XBIT.adversarial: The house case: the floor MELTS ~$36M/yr (~33% of net cash annually) on clinical burn, so a ~3yr runway; the recurring capital return is real but UNDATED and management could instead spend the cash on trials; and there is no live tender today. A melting floor with a dormant catalyst is a value bet, not a sure thing.
citations: SEC 10-Q XBiotech Inc, filed 2026-05-14, https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1626878/000117184326003419/xbit20260331_10q.htm: cash $115.46M, total liabilities $4.82M, ZERO debt/leases/preferred; 30,487,731 sh; prior tenders SC TO-I 2020-01-14 & 2023-05-17, special dividend 8-K 2021-07-06.

verification.notes: Primary-source verified (Q1-2026 10-Q): $115.5M cash, zero debt/leases/preferred, all options OTM. 36.6% below net cash (25% FD). Floor HARD but melts ~$36M/yr; recurring capital-return history is the catalyst but dormant. Size as bounded-floor value bet.

SEER.author: oracle
SEER.business: Seer, Inc. — proteomics tools company (Proteograph platform for deep, unbiased plasma proteome analysis).
SEER.thesis: Seer holds $219.5M of liquid net cash (cash + Treasuries/CP, zero real debt) against a ~$111M market cap — trading 25-49% below net cash even fully diluted and net of leases. The market prices it on its pre-scale tools revenue and losses; meanwhile the company is BUYING BACK stock, shrinking the count into the discount. The screen actually understated the floor by excluding $52.8M of long-term Treasuries.
SEER.why_mispriced: Uncovered pre-scale tools company priced on its P&L losses, not its $219.5M liquid, debt-free balance sheet; the market ignores that buybacks are shrinking the share count into the discount.
SEER.catalyst: Ongoing share buyback (accretive below net cash) shrinking the count; a proteomics-adoption inflection; or activist/strategic pressure. A stockholder rights plan (2026-02) may cap a takeover premium.
SEER.falsifiable_prediction: Within ~18 months SEER re-rates toward net cash (>= $2.60/sh) OR buybacks + a stable floor hold >= $2.60.
SEER.kill_condition: Net cash per share falls below $2.20 (burn outpaces the buyback), or a dilutive raise.
SEER.kill_condition_value: 10-Q net cash/sh < $2.20 or a dilutive equity raise
SEER.adversarial: The house case: the floor melts ~$62M/yr (~28% of net cash) on tools-business burn (~3.5yr runway); the poison pill caps any acquisition premium; the buyback is small vs the discount; and the proteomics adoption thesis is unproven. Real cash, but a melting floor with a soft catalyst.
citations: SEC 10-Q Seer Inc, filed 2026-05-13, accession 0001193125-26-221724: cash $47.3M + ST investments $119.4M + LT investments $52.8M = $219.5M liquid; zero real debt; Class A 54.98M sh (Class B 0); buybacks reducing count; Stockholder Rights Plan 8-K 2026-02-26.

verification.notes: Primary-source verified (Q1-2026 10-Q): $219.5M liquid net cash (incl $52.8M LT Treasuries the screen missed), zero real debt, buying back stock. 25-49% below net cash. Floor HARD but melts ~$62M/yr; poison pill caps takeover premium. Bounded-floor value bet.

LAB.author: oracle
LAB.business: Standard BioTools Inc. (fka Fluidigm) — life-science tools (mass cytometry / microfluidics); divested SomaScan proteomics to Illumina (Jan 2026).
LAB.thesis: Standard BioTools holds ~$526M of cash + marketable securities against a ~$356M market cap — ~20% below net cash fully diluted (32% on shares outstanding) with effectively zero debt and, critically, NO senior preferred: the feared Casdin/Viking convertible preferred already converted to common. The Jan-2026 SomaScan divestiture to Illumina bolstered the pile; the market prices only the unprofitable residual tools business.
LAB.why_mispriced: Post-divestiture cash-rich tools company priced on its money-losing residual business; the market assumes a phantom senior-preferred stack that has already converted to common, so the debt-free $526M liquid floor is unencumbered and uncounted.
LAB.catalyst: Capital return, an accretive M&A use of the cash, or a strategic sale/liquidation of the residual tools business. The $50M buyback authorization expired 2026-03; a renewed return would close the gap.
LAB.falsifiable_prediction: Within ~24 months LAB re-rates toward net cash (>= $1.05/sh) OR the debt-free net-cash floor holds >= $1.05 while price lags.
LAB.kill_condition: Net cash per fully-diluted share falls below $0.95 (residual-business burn outpaces the pile), or a value-destroying acquisition.
LAB.kill_condition_value: 10-Q net cash/FD share < $0.95 or a dilutive/value-destroying M&A
LAB.adversarial: The house case: the residual mass-cytometry/microfluidics business is unprofitable and burns ~$20-30M/qtr normalized (~19%/yr of the pile, 4-5yr runway); the share count is enormous (~390M) from prior massive dilution; the buyback has expired; and there is no dated catalyst — a melting floor needing a capital-return/M&A trigger, not a sure re-rate.
citations: SEC 10-Q Standard BioTools Inc, filed 2026-05-06, accession 0001193125-26-208967: cash $265.8M + ST investments $189.4M + LT marketable $71.4M = $526.5M; convertible notes $0.3M; ZERO preferred outstanding (Casdin/Viking converted); 390.1M common; SomaScan sale to Illumina closed 2026-01-30 (net $363.2M).

verification.notes: Primary-source verified (Q1-2026 10-Q): $526.5M liquid net cash, $0.3M debt, ZERO preferred (feared Casdin/Viking stack already converted). ~20% FD / 32% shares-out below net cash. Floor HARD but melts ~19%/yr; buyback expired, needs a catalyst. Bounded-floor value bet.

FPH.author: oracle
FPH.business: Five Point Holdings, LLC — California master-planned-community land developer (Valencia, San Francisco Candlestick/Shipyard, and a 37.5% stake in the Great Park Venture in Irvine); ~21,000 Valencia + ~11,800 Great Park entitled homesites.
FPH.thesis: Five Point trades at ~0.45x a book that ITSELF understates its California land: the 37.5% Great Park stake is carried at $99.9M equity-method book, yet that venture sold 920 Irvine homesites for $781.7M and distributed $252M cash to FPH in 2025 alone (~44% of the market cap). The market ignores the land NAV because of Up-C/NCI structural opacity, micro-cap illiquidity, and lumpy episodic realization that deters institutions.
FPH.why_mispriced: Structural neglect of a land bank: Up-C/NCI complexity + micro-cap illiquidity + lumpy JV-distribution cadence make screens read the quarterly air-pockets, not the entitled California land carried at decades-old cost far below transacting value.
FPH.catalyst: Recurring Great Park Venture distributions ($252M to FPH in 2025), ~$100M 2026 net-income guidance, and an activist (Robotti & Co, 9.7% Class A SC 13D) pushing on capitalization/M&A.
FPH.falsifiable_prediction: Within ~24 months FPH re-rates toward book-at-cost (>= $9/sh) as Great Park distributions continue, OR the land floor holds >= $9/sh.
FPH.kill_condition: Great Park homesite pricing falls materially (sub-$500k/homesite), a distribution halt, or net debt rises above ~$400M (leverage-up on a bad deal).
FPH.kill_condition_value: Great Park homesite price < $500k, distribution halt, or net debt > $400M
FPH.adversarial: The house case: realization is LUMPY and slow (Q1'26 had $0 land sales, −$44.5M operating cash) and FPH controls only 37.5% of the crown-jewel Great Park Venture (75% approval to act) — it does NOT dictate the pace; the SF Candlestick/Shipyard land is environmentally encumbered and speculative; Lennar + Castlelake hold ~35% of the OpCo (overhang); and the Up-C/NCI structure makes per-share NAV imprecise. A land bank can stay cheap for years.
citations: SEC 10-Q Five Point Holdings, filed 2026-04-24, accession 0001574197-26-000020: inventory at cost $2,476.4M, cash $332.6M, senior notes net $443.7M (8% due 2030), members' capital $842.0M, NCI $1,465.5M, Class A 72.3M / Class B 76.1M (147.4M fully exchanged).
SEC 10-K FY2025 + earnings 8-K accession 0001574197-26-000002: net income $183.5M, EPS $1.01, Great Park 920 homesites / $781.7M / $252.0M distribution, $100M 2026 guidance.
SC 13D Robotti & Co accession 0001140361-24-042124 (9.66% Class A).

verification.notes: BLIND fresh-run find (2026-07-06). Verified against Q1-2026 10-Q + FY2025 10-K/8-K: land at cost $2,476M, only ~$111M net debt, $643M liquidity, profitable ($183.5M FY25 NI). Book-at-cost floor ~$16/sh understates the land (Great Park $99.9M book vs ~$849k/homesite transacting); catalyst is demonstrated (recurring Great Park cash) + an activist 13D. Size PATIENT — lumpy, non-controlled realization.
